pillar: Reducing the cost of building AI systems
title: CRUCIBLE
description: A multi-agent framework that automatically calibrates annotation rubrics by diagnosing disagreement between LLM judges, replacing manual rubric engineering with a converging iterative loop. Raised inter-judge agreement from chance-level to as high as 96.7% on real annotation data. Currently under review; its methodology underlies the published SNR-Detector paper.

pillar: Enabling AI to run outside centralized infrastructure
title: SNR-Detector
description: A binary classification framework that scores educational video content by genuine signal versus noise, using transcript embeddings and a lightweight classifier that runs independently of a hosted LLM API. Achieves F1 = 0.871 with 0.964 recall on high-signal content. A local-classifier extension is in progress.

title: Essence
description: A personal embedding-cluster framework for music recommendation that models individual taste directly from listening history, improving long-tail recovery without fine-tuning the underlying model.

title: Asterism
description: A forthcoming tool extending the personalization work in Essence to a broader class of recommendation problems. Publishing publicly in the coming weeks.
